# PullRequestService.spec:250 flake — getPullRequestDiff file parameter filter under workers:1

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-08 during PR #10940 (TestLifecycleHelper substrate migration) Cycle 1 review. Empirical full-unit-suite run (`CI=true npm run test-unit`, matches CI workers:1 substrate) showed `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/PullRequestService.spec.mjs:250` flakes — passes on retry. This is a NEW surface, not in the prior Bucket G G5 triage matrix.

## The Problem

Test `getPullRequestDiff (#10748) › file parameter filters the diff output` (line 250) flakes under workers:1. Specific failure shape needs empirical capture from a fresh CI run + log inspection — my Cycle 1 review ran the suite but the flake's first-attempt error wasn't pulled into evidence (Playwright reports flakes as "passed eventually" in summary; the failed attempt's trace is in test-results/ but not pulled).

**Triage hypotheses (not yet empirically verified):**
1. **Singleton state pollution from sibling github-workflow specs** — DiscussionService.spec, ConfigCompleteness.spec, IssueService.spec, etc. all live under `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow` and may share GraphqlService or similar singleton state. Same root-cause class as G5 patterns elsewhere.
2. **Race in GraphqlService.query mocking** — the test likely overrides `GraphqlService.query` for the test scenario; under fullyParallel-ish ordering with workers:1, sibling spec's cleanup may not restore the original `query` binding cleanly.
3. **Genuine race in `getPullRequestDiff` file-parameter filtering logic** — if hypothesis 1+2 both fail, this is an actual production race.

## The Architectural Reality

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/PullRequestService.spec.mjs:250` — the flaky test (`getPullRequestDiff #10748 file parameter filters the diff output`)
- `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/services/PullRequestService.mjs#getPullRequestDiff` — the production target (added in #10748)
- Sibling github-workflow specs that share GraphqlService:
  - `DiscussionService.spec.mjs` (G5#1, fixed via #10929)
  - `ConfigCompleteness.spec.mjs` (G4 cascade, fixed via #10929)
  - `IssueService.spec.mjs` (Zod validator surface)

## The Fix (TBD via investigation)

Investigation steps:
1. Pull the actual failure trace from a CI run — `gh run view ... --log-failed` for the first-attempt error
2. Categorize: GraphqlService mock leak vs new substrate vs production race
3. Implement targeted fix; coordinate with @neo-gpt if it's another github-workflow Config namespace cascade (his G4 lane)

Skip-guarded in Phase 3 PR (forthcoming) per NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI pattern; this ticket tracks the proper investigation + fix.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** Pull empirical failure trace from a CI run (the first-attempt error before retry passes)
- [ ] **(AC2)** Categorize root cause hypothesis (singleton-pollution / mock-leak / production-race)
- [ ] **(AC3)** Implement targeted fix
- [ ] **(AC4)** Remove the NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guard from PullRequestService.spec.mjs:250 in Phase 3 PR (forthcoming)
- [ ] **(AC5)** Verify PullRequestService.spec:250 runs deterministically across 5 consecutive `CI=true npm run test-unit` invocations

## Out of Scope

- Migrating PullRequestService.spec to TestLifecycleHelper unconditionally (unknown if applicable until hypothesis is identified)
- Rewriting `getPullRequestDiff` (out of scope unless hypothesis 3 holds)

## Avoided Traps

- **Pre-emptive TestLifecycleHelper migration**: rejected without empirical hypothesis evidence. Migration is the right shape for singleton-state-pollution but unnecessary if the shape is mock-leak or production race.
- **Skip-guard as permanent solution**: applied as immediate Phase 3 ship-the-PR move + tracked here for proper fix.

## Related

- **Surfacing CI run:** my empirical CI=true full-suite run on PR #10940 c.7f454ee2 (1055 passed, 4 flaky)
- **Cycle 1 review citation:** [PR #10940 commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBodQ9A](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10940#issuecomment-4404498676) — listed as new surface not in prior triage
- **Production target:** #10748 (`getPullRequestDiff` introduced)
- **Sibling github-workflow flake patterns:** #10929 G4 namespace collision (resolved), G5#1 cascade (resolved)
- **Bucket G epic:** #10924

GPT's Cycle 1 review on c.ea2e52066 surfaced 4 additional residual flakes
that my prior empirical run on PR #10940 branch missed (workers:1 + test-order
randomness produces different flake-set per run):

- GraphService.spec:158 (`getContextFrontier topology`) — HARD FAIL
- GraphService.spec:172 (`SQLite lazy-load on cache miss`)  — HARD FAIL
- IssueService.spec:253 (dispatcher validation)            — flaky
- TransportService.spec:23 (G5#4 onsessionclosed)          — flaky

All same root-cause class as the existing skip-guards. Adding 4 more guards:

- GraphService.spec:158 + :172 → #10941 (extend prior :107 tracker scope)
- IssueService.spec:253        → #10942 (github-workflow GraphqlService mock pollution)
- TransportService.spec:23     → #10935 (G5#4 — pre-existing tracker)

Note on iteration discipline: this is Cycle 2 of Phase 3 (after the 5-cycle
PR #10933 cascade closed Drop+Supersede). Per `feedback_substrate_scope_restraint`,
if this push doesn't converge to green, the right move is another scope-restraint
+ wait for deeper substrate fix (extension of TestLifecycleHelper migration to
github-workflow specs OR SDK-level open-state defensive checks)."

- 2026-05-08T11:05:09Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `5f1ac1f` - "test(ci): add skip-guards for residual Bucket G flakes (#10939)

Addresses Gemini's PR #10953 Cycle 1 review (commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBpwL4A).
The PR body claimed skip-guards were in place but the diff was workflow-only —
the skip-guards themselves were missing. CI's `unit` row failed accordingly.

Adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI test.skip() at the top of each named flake's body, citing
the tracking ticket so removal is mechanical when the proper fix lands:

- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec.mjs:78
  → #10946 (synthesis-write race post-#10940 engine=hybrid fix)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:92
  → #10936 (kb_query_log singleton-data pollution)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:159
  → #10941 (workers:1 close-singleton residual)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:174
  → #10941 (same root-cause class as :159)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:182
  → #10937 (AGENT:* singleton-data pollution under workers:1)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/IssueService.spec.mjs:254
  → bucket G #10924 (GraphqlService mock-pollution residual; sub-ticket
  TBD — same root-cause class as #10942 PullRequestService:250 but distinct
  surface. Cite epic for now; sub-ticket if root cause diverges)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs:24
  → #10935 (HTTP bind race residual post-#10930 fix)

GraphService.spec.mjs:108 and :136 already had the bucket-G3 skip-guard from
prior cycles; left untouched. All 7 new guards follow the existing canonical
pattern (`test.skip(!!process.env.NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI, 'CI-skip: <reason> (#N)')`)
established in #10907/#10921/#10928.

Per Gemini's review §Test-Execution & Location Audit + Required Actions, this
closes both bullets:
1. Skip-guards added for the 4 PR-body-named flakes
2. Skip-guards added for the 4 additional flakes Gemini caught in the CI run
   (GraphService:158/172, IssueService:253, TransportService:23)

- 2026-05-08T12:43:50Z @tobiu referenced in commit `98897fc` - "feat(ci): re-add unit suite to matrix post-Bucket-G substrate (#10939) (#10953)

* feat(ci): re-add unit suite to matrix post-Bucket-G substrate (#10939)

Bucket A-F audit (#10903) closed via per-bucket fixes (#10907 / #10921 /
#10910 / #10919 / #10920). Bucket G (#10924) substrate fixes landed via
PR #10940 (TestLifecycleHelper primitive + daemon-spec migration) plus
its follow-up #10944 (HealthService sqlite-vec artifact cleanup).

Residual Bucket G flakes remain tracked in their own sub-tickets — all
skip-guarded via NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI:
- #10941 — GraphService.spec:107 residual flake (hypothesis-2 falsified
  empirically; investigation tilts toward hypothesis 1 hook-scope or
  hypothesis 3 SDK lazy re-init contract)
- #10936 — KBRecorderService.spec:91 (kb_query_log singleton-data
  pollution, distinct root cause class)
- #10937 — PermissionService.spec:181 (AGENT:* singleton-data pollution)
- #10946 — DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec:77 (synthesis-write race
  unmasked by the engine='neo' to engine='hybrid' fix in #10940)

Empirical validation: 5-iteration AC4-strict run on origin/dev at
a771afdb0 produced identical composition each iteration (16 hard fail
+ 4-5 flaky + 3 did-not-run + 1056 passed, with the flake set above
deterministic 4/5 plus SessionSummarization:114 intermittent 2/5 which
is bucket-A skip-guarded in CI). FileSystemIngestor.spec (#10934) and
Database.spec (#10938) — the original Bucket G G6 cascade surfaces —
appear in zero failure / flake / did-not-run lists across all 5 runs.

Workflow edit: matrix `suite: [integration]` -> `suite: [integration,
unit]`. Comment block updated to reflect Bucket A-G substrate state and
the canonical NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI bucket-skip pattern (env unchanged from
prior workflow row).

Predecessor PRs that cycled and closed Drop+Supersede per
substrate-scope-restraint discipline:
- PR #10933 (5 cycles) - skip-guard whack-a-mole pre-substrate-fix
- PR #10943 (2 cycles) - same pattern, narrower scope

Both demonstrated empirically that workers:1 substrate flakes do not
converge linearly via skip-guards alone. Substrate fix landed first
(PR #10940); this PR is a clean workflow-line addition gated on the
substrate state, not iterative skip-guard cycling.

* test(ci): add skip-guards for residual Bucket G flakes (#10939)

Addresses Gemini's PR #10953 Cycle 1 review (commentId IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBpwL4A).
The PR body claimed skip-guards were in place but the diff was workflow-only —
the skip-guards themselves were missing. CI's `unit` row failed accordingly.

the tracking ticket so removal is mechanical when the proper fix lands:

- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec.mjs:78
  → #10946 (synthesis-write race post-#10940 engine=hybrid fix)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:92
  → #10936 (kb_query_log singleton-data pollution)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:159

- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:174
  → #10941 (same root-cause class as :159)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/PermissionService.spec.mjs:182
  → #10937 (AGENT:* singleton-data pollution under workers:1)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/IssueService.spec.mjs:254
  → bucket G #10924 (GraphqlService mock-pollution residual; sub-ticket
  TBD — same root-cause class as #10942 PullRequestService:250 but distinct
  surface. Cite epic for now; sub-ticket if root cause diverges)
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs:24
  → #10935 (HTTP bind race residual post-#10930 fix)

GraphService.spec.mjs:108 and :136 already had the bucket-G3 skip-guard from
prior cycles; left untouched. All 7 new guards follow the existing canonical

Per Gemini's review §Test-Execution & Location Audit + Required Actions, this

* test(ci): add skip-guards for GraphService.spec:242 + :284 residual flakes (#10939)

CI run after `5f1ac1fa9` surfaced 2 additional GraphService.spec hard failures
that were pass-on-retry-flaky in my local 5-run baseline but flipped to
hard-fail under CI's specific spec-interleaving order:

- `GraphService.spec.mjs:242` ('should recover from boot-time identity cache
  race (stuck vicinity cache)') → workers:1 close-singleton residual (#10941)
- `GraphService.spec.mjs:284` ('should lazy-load topology for getContextFrontier
  when frontiers drop out of cache') → same root-cause class as :242 + :107
  + :158 + :172 (#10941)

Both tests follow the same vicinity-cache + lazy-load-from-SQLite pattern
that exposes the close-singleton-then-reuse race tracked in #10941. Skip-guard
cites the existing tracker — same removal mechanic as the prior 5 GraphService
guards (#10941 covers all 5 surface variants).

This is the second iteration on the Phase 3 re-add post-#10940 substrate. Per
`feedback_substrate_scope_restraint`: NOT cycling on iterative skip-guards via
the same PR (predecessor #10933/#10943 cycle pattern). Each commit on this PR
addresses a specific empirical CI signal — distinct from the iterative
whack-a-mole shape that triggered Drop+Supersede on the prior cycle.

Co-Authored-By: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>

* test(ci): add skip-guards for GraphService.spec:315 + :356 (#10939)

Gemini's PR #10953 Cycle 3 review flagged 2 more GraphService.spec hard
failures from CI on commit 4c2987c4c — both same workers:1 close-singleton
residual root-cause class as :107/:158/:172/:174/:242/:284 (all under #10941).

Per @tobiu's directive: keep iterating skip-guards until CI is green; ~95%
working in the GH workflow with some skipped tests is the canonical pattern
(established #10907/#10921/#10928 + cohort). Drop+Supersede was an
over-application of `feedback_substrate_scope_restraint` for unit-row re-add
work specifically — substrate-scope-restraint applies to substrate-fix
iteration cycles, not to skip-guard application on already-substrate-fixed
work.

- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:316
  ('should execute getPaths and lazy-load recursively across deep dependencies
   when RAM cache is missed') → #10941
- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs:357
  ('should automatically execute LRU garbage collection Native Graph footprints
   when maxGraphNodes is exceeded') → #10941

PR #10953 reopened (was closed Drop+Supersede prematurely). This commit
continues the iteration.

Co-Authored-By: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>
